content: Canvas2D mobile perf cliffs: (1) Gradients created per-element per-frame are expensive — pre-cache CanvasGradient objects in a map keyed by parameters; (2) float coordinates force sub-pixel anti-aliasing — Math.floor all coordinates; (3) devicePixelRatio > 2 (common on modern phones at 3x) means canvas physical size is 3x viewport, tripling GPU fill; cap at 2 or use CSS scaling; (4) Overdraw from layered alpha fills costs GPU bandwidth — batch opaque fills before transparent; (5) OffscreenCanvas + Web Worker only helps if main thread is the bottleneck — for 60fps freeze-and-cache strategy, bake frozen shells to OffscreenCanvas once and drawImage in subsequent frames; (6) Dynamic gradients and canvas state changes (fillStyle, strokeStyle, lineWidth) are expensive to thrash — group draws by style; (7) Exceeding ~1000 dynamic objects per frame needs OffscreenCanvas Worker.
